title: About Me - Natalie Fleet MP | Bolsover
meta_description: Find out more about my work, campaigns and how to help make our local area better for everyone.
text_excerpt: About Me - Natalie Fleet MP | Bolsover 0207 219 5665 Email Me HOME ABOUT ME NEWS USEFUL LINKS HELP Contact Visit Parliament Event Request SURGERIES ABOUT ME Find out more about my work, campaigns and how to help make our local area better for everyone. I’m Natalie Fleet — a mum, a Labour MP, a trade unionist, and someone who knows what it’s like to struggle to get by in Tory Britain. Back in 2010, life looked very different. I worked in a busy inner-city advice centre, helping people in some of the most difficult circumstances. My husband was a plumber, working hard and earning a fair wage. Our children went to a Sure Start nursery and a properly funded local school with happy, supported staff. We could afford a family holiday. I didn’t lie awake at night worrying about the cost of food or the gas bill. But over the years, like many families, we felt the squeeze. I lost my job due to cuts in the voluntary sector. The Sure Start centre shut down. My husband’s work became unpredictable. And despite working hard, we were left feeling like it was never enough, as wages fell and prices rose. We’re strivers, and we kept striving — but the system kept pushing back. I wasn’t born into politics. I was born into a proud working-class community. My dad was a miner, my mum worked in a factory, and like most people around us, we voted Labour because it stood for fairness, dignity and opportunity. I became a mum in my final year of school. And it was only looking back that I realised how much the Labour government helped me — not by doing everything for me, but by giving me the support I needed to keep going. Thanks to things like bursaries, quality childcare and the minimum wage, I became the first in my family to go to university. I wasn’t a scrounger. I worked hard. But I needed help, and I was given it. That’s what politics can do at its best. Now I’m proud beyond words to be the Member of Parliament for Bolsover. And I’m using my voice in Parliament to raise the issues that matter to us, because I know we deserve more. I’m fighting to make sure Bolsover gets the funding and investment our towns and villages need. That mineworkers finally get the justice they deserve. That we fix broken bus services and improve public transport links. That young people have more opportunities close to home, and families with children who have special educational needs get proper support. I’m pushing for better 5G and broadband coverage, safer roads and fewer potholes. Basic things that make a big difference. In Parliament, I ask questions, speak in debates, vote on laws and raise our concerns in the chamber — always with Bolsover in mind. This is the community that raised me, and I’ll never stop speaking up for it.
